skill.name: cal-com-automation
skill.description: Automate Cal.com tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): manage bookings, check availability, configure webhooks, and handle teams.
skill.summary: Automate Cal.com scheduling operations through Composio's Cal toolkit via Rube MCP for booking management and calendar integration.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is a documentation file (SKILL.md) that describes how to use Cal.com automation via Rube MCP. All 64 static analyzer findings are false positives: markdown backticks used for code formatting were incorrectly flagged as Ruby shell execution, documentation URLs were flagged as network access, and parameter descriptions were flagged as environment access. No executable code is present in this file.

content.value_statement: Managing Cal.com bookings manually is time-consuming. This skill automates booking management, availability checking, webhook configuration, and team operations through Rube MCP.
actual_capabilities: List and filter bookings by status, date range, and other criteria
Create new bookings by finding available slots and submitting requests
Check calendar availability and busy time blocks for date ranges
Configure webhooks for booking events like creation, rescheduling, and cancellation
Manage teams including creation, retrieval, and team event types
Retrieve organization ID for org-level operations
limitations: Requires active Rube MCP connection with Cal.com toolkit configured
Cannot bypass Cal.com API rate limits or authentication requirements
Team and organization operations require appropriate permissions
Does not work with personal Cal.com accounts that lack organization features

use_cases.title: Automated Booking Assistant
use_cases.description: Help users find available meeting slots and create bookings without manual calendar checking.
use_cases.target_user: Executive assistants and coordinators managing multiple schedules

use_cases.title: Webhook Integration Setup
use_cases.description: Configure Cal.com webhooks to trigger notifications in external systems when bookings change.
use_cases.target_user: Developers integrating Cal.com with CRM or notification systems

use_cases.title: Team Scheduling Management
use_cases.description: Create and manage team event types for group bookings and collaborative scheduling.
use_cases.target_user: Team leads and HR managers organizing team events

prompt_templates.title: View upcoming bookings
prompt_templates.prompt: List all my upcoming Cal.com bookings for the next two weeks.

prompt_templates.title: Find available meeting slots
prompt_templates.prompt: Check available slots for event type [ID] between [start date] and [end date] in [timezone].

prompt_templates.title: Create a new booking
prompt_templates.prompt: Book a meeting for [attendee name] at [email] on [date] at [time] for event type [ID].

prompt_templates.title: Set up booking notifications
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure a webhook to send notifications to [URL] when bookings are created or cancelled.

output_examples.output:
Found 3 upcoming bookings:
- Team Standup with John (Jan 15, 10:00 AM EST)
- Client Demo with Acme Corp (Jan 16, 2:00 PM PST)
- 1:1 with Manager (Jan 17, 3:00 PM EST)

output_examples.input: Check availability for event type 123 next week
output_examples.output:
Available slots for Consultation (30 min):
- Monday Jan 15: 9:00 AM, 11:00 AM, 3:00 PM
- Tuesday Jan 16: 10:00 AM, 2:00 PM, 4:00 PM
- Wednesday Jan 17: 9:00 AM, 1:00 PM

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before executing workflows
Verify Rube MCP connection status is ACTIVE before running any Cal.com operations
Specify timezone explicitly in all date/time parameters to avoid scheduling confusion
anti_patterns: Do not create bookings without first checking available slots for the event type
Do not use date ranges spanning months for availability checks as results may be inaccurate
Do not assume team or organization access - verify permissions before operations

faq.question: What do I need to set up before using this skill?
faq.answer: You need Rube MCP configured with an active Cal.com connection. Add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server, then authenticate Cal.com through RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS.

faq.question: How do I find my event type ID?
faq.answer: Use CAL_GET_AVAILABLE_SLOTS_INFO or check your Cal.com dashboard. Event type IDs are numeric integers that identify specific meeting configurations.

faq.question: Can I use this with a personal Cal.com account?
faq.answer: Basic booking operations work with personal accounts. However, team and organization management features require a Cal.com organization plan.

faq.question: What date formats should I use?
faq.answer: Use ISO 8601 format with timezone for booking times (2024-01-15T09:00:00Z) and YYYY-MM-DD format for availability date ranges.

faq.question: How do webhook notifications work?
faq.answer: Webhooks send HTTP POST requests to your endpoint when specified events occur. You need a publicly accessible HTTPS URL to receive notifications.

faq.question: What happens if I hit Cal.com API rate limits?
faq.answer: Cal.com returns 429 status on rate limit. The skill should implement exponential backoff and retry after the specified delay.
